repo.description: Vim-fork focused on extensibility and usability
repo.name: neovim

body:
## Problem:
If NVIM_LOG_FILE, or the default fallback, is inaccessible (e.g. directory is owned by root), users get confused.

## Solution:
- Show a warning when $NVIM_LOG_FILE or $XDG_STATE_HOME are inaccessible.
- Also fix a latent memory leak: `os_mkdir_recurse` returns a uv error code (int), but it was stored as `bool`, causing `os_strerror` to receive an invalid error code and leak memory.


---

implements justinmk proposal in #38039. 

closes #38039

comment_data.body: just use regular "/" slashes. zero need for `\` slashes. and this shouldn't be needed anyway, `'/foo/bar'` should also work on Windows.
comment_data.diff_hunk:
@@ -99,4 +99,30 @@ describe('log', function()
     -- Child Nvim spawned by jobstart() prepends "c/" to parent name.
     assert_log('c/' .. tid .. '%.%d+%.%d +server_init:%d+: test log message', testlog, 100)
   end)
+
+  it('warns when NVIM_LOG_FILE is inaccessible', function()
+    local invalid_path = '/foo/bar'
+    if t.is_os('win') then
+      invalid_path = 'C:\\invalid\\path\\nvim.log'

```suggestion

```

Probably for the best, especially as the `+1` was forgotten in the first commit. (`sizeof` works too, as it's `char`)
I'd maybe consider matching more of the message here, to ensure `$DEFAULT_NVIM_LOG_FILE` is being set correctly? (if not too fiddly to do)

@@ -99,4 +99,24 @@ describe('log', function()
     -- Child Nvim spawned by jobstart() prepends "c/" to parent name.
     assert_log('c/' .. tid .. '%.%d+%.%d +server_init:%d+: test log message', testlog, 100)
   end)
+

+    clear({
+      args_rm = { '-u' },
+      args = { '--clean' },

+    t.matches('not accessible', n.exec_capture('messages'))
+  end)

+  it('warns when NVIM_LOG_FILE and $XDG_STATE_HOME is inaccessible', function()

+    clear({
+      args_rm = { '-u' },
+      args = { '--clean' },
+      env = { NVIM_LOG_FILE = invalid_path, XDG_STATE_HOME = invalid_path },
+    })
+    t.matches('not accessible', n.exec_capture('messages'))

comment_data.body:
~~env vars are yuck. how about setting a variable in this module, e.g.~~

```lua
require('vim._core.util').foo = '...'
```

To avoid depending on Lua from `log.c`, env var is acceptable. And besides, this env var is (1) short-lived and (2) only happens in rare circumstances.
comment_data.diff_hunk:
@@ -81,4 +81,30 @@ function M.source_is_lua(bufnr, line1, line2)
   return lang_tree:lang() == 'lua'
 end
 
+function M._warn_log_file_fallback()
+  local mode = vim.fn.mode()
+  if mode == 'c' then -- in es mode
+    return
+  end
+
+  local default_log_file = vim.fn.getenv('DEFAULT_NVIM_LOG_FILE')

comment_data.body: instead of doing this, and instead of trying to call Lua from log.c (not wanted), return a boolean from `log_init` so that `main.c` can do something with it after it calls `nlua_init`

A boolean would only tell us that a fallback happened, not which path was inaccessible. Setting the module variable directly seems to me like the cleanest solution: 
```lua
nlua_exec("require('vim._core.util')._default_log_file = ...", default_log_file_path);
```
Is this acceptable?

```suggestion

  -- Warn if $NVIM_LOG_FILE or $XDG_STATE_HOME are inaccessible. #38039
```

```suggestion

```
comment_data.diff_hunk:
@@ -81,6 +81,32 @@ function M.source_is_lua(bufnr, line1, line2)
   return lang_tree:lang() == 'lua'
 end
 
+<<<<<<< fix/warn-nvim-log-file-inaccessible
+function M._warn_log_file_fallback()
+  local mode = vim.fn.mode()
+  if mode == 'c' then -- in es mode
+    return
+  end
+
+  local default_log_file = vim.fn.getenv('DEFAULT_NVIM_LOG_FILE')
+  if default_log_file == vim.NIL then
+    return
+  end
+
+  local log_file = vim.fn.getenv('NVIM_LOG_FILE')
+
+  if log_file == vim.NIL or log_file == '' then
+    vim.notify(
+      ('%q is not accessible, logging disabled (stderr)'):format(default_log_file),
+      vim.log.levels.WARN
+    )
+  elseif log_file ~= default_log_file then
+    vim.notify(
+      ('%q is not accessible, logging to: %q'):format(default_log_file, log_file),
+      vim.log.levels.WARN
+    )
+  end
+=======

comment_data.body: On reflection, to avoid depending on Lua from `log.c`, env var is acceptable. And besides, this env var is (1) short-lived and (2) only happens in rare circumstances.
Do we actually need `default_log_file_path` or could we just call `os_setenv` when needed?
Nice catch, thanks for explaining in the commit message.
when trying this locally, the warning doesn't actually show on startup (because of a redraw or something). using `defer_fn` to workaround that for now.
comment_data.diff_hunk:
@@ -0,0 +1,30 @@
+local M = {}
+
+--- Checks that the logfile is accessible.
+function M.check_log_file()
+  if vim.fn.mode() == 'c' then -- Ex mode
+    return
+  end
+
+  local wanted = vim.fn.getenv('__NVIM_LOG_FILE_WANT')
+  if not wanted or wanted == vim.NIL then
+    return
+  end
+
+  local actual = vim.fn.getenv('NVIM_LOG_FILE')
+
+  local msg = nil
+  if not actual or actual == vim.NIL or actual == '' then
+    msg = ('log: %q not accessible, logging disabled (stderr)'):format(wanted)
+  elseif actual ~= wanted then
+    msg = ('log: %q not accessible, logging to: %q'):format(wanted, actual)
+  else
+    return
+  end
+
+  vim.defer_fn(function()
+    vim.notify(msg, vim.log.levels.WARN)
+  end, 100)

comment_data.body: Iam unable to recreate the issue. Ill leave it with the vim.defer_fn anyways as it is more robust.
comment_data.diff_hunk:
@@ -0,0 +1,30 @@

+  elseif actual ~= wanted then

+  vim.defer_fn(function()
+    vim.notify(msg, vim.log.levels.WARN)
+  end, 100)

comment_data.body: Key idea: `__NVIM_LOG_FILE_WANT` is set *only* in the failure case(s). We don't want to define it in the normal case.

```suggestion
  local msg --[[@type string]]
```
comment_data.diff_hunk:
@@ -0,0 +1,30 @@
+local M = {}
+
+--- Checks that the logfile is accessible.

+  if not wanted or wanted == vim.NIL then
+    return
+  end
+
+  local actual = vim.fn.getenv('NVIM_LOG_FILE')
+
+  local msg = nil --luacheck: ignore

comment_regular_data.body:
@justinmk 
would running the check on `VimEnter` autocmd be a better choice here? as we don't need to change anything in `main.c` unlike #27537, and it will prevent `windows (old)` from failing as much as i understand it.

> would running the check on `VimEnter` autocmd be a better choice here?

yes, then the impl can be fully in Lua. but the question is how will the VimEnter handler know when to warn.

> prevent `windows (old)` from failing 

    RunTheTest[61]..Test_issue_3969 line 6: Expected 'hello' but got '"C:\\\\Users\\\\runneradmin\\\\AppData\\\\Local\\\\nvim-data\\\\nvim.log" is not accessible, logging to: "Xnvim.log"hello'

look at the test implementation to understand why that happens.
https://github.com/neovim/neovim/blob/5cbb9d613bf6d8983b5effd7e20343a4ef497c06/test/old/testdir/test_startup.vim#L828

so the reason test / ubuntu asan clang functionaltest (pull_request) was failing is because of a latent memory bug that @seandewar helped me solve.

the bug happened because log_dir_failure was declared as of type bool when it should have been of type int because it holds a uv error code. so as documented in https://docs.libuv.org/en/v1.x/errors.html#c.uv_strerror it would leak memory.

i've also fixed windows (old) by checking if it was running in es mode and not raise a warning if it was.
the pr is ready for merging.

> is there a different way this could be made available to the warning message?

i thought about doing 

```c
nlua_exec("vim._default_log_file = ...", default_log_file_path);
``` 
then we can
```lua
local default_log_file = vim._default_log_file
```
not a hard change.  will wait for Justin's opinion.

title: fix(log): warn when NVIM_LOG_FILE is inaccessible
